task: Research GitHub Marketplace submission process for AI agents

findings.overview: GitHub Marketplace allows developers to list tools that integrate with GitHub, including GitHub Apps, GitHub Actions, and OAuth Apps. An AI agent can be listed as a GitHub App or a GitHub Action.

listing_categories.description: A first-class integration that acts on behalf of the app itself or a user. Suitable for AI agents that need to interact with GitHub APIs (e.g., reviewing PRs, managing issues, running AI analysis on code).
key_requirements: Must be registered as a GitHub App in GitHub Developer Settings
Must have a public or private listing on GitHub Marketplace
Must subscribe to webhook events as needed
Must provide a callback URL (for OAuth flow if user-to-server)
Must implement secure webhook secret verification

listing_categories.description: A reusable automation unit that runs on GitHub's CI/CD platform (GitHub Actions). Suitable for AI agents that perform batch analysis, code generation, or CI/CD integration.
key_requirements: Must be a public repository with an action.yml or action.yaml file
Must have a README with clear usage documentation
Must be published to GitHub Marketplace via the Actions tab
Must define inputs, outputs, and runtime (Docker, Node.js, Python, etc.)
Must use semantic versioning for releases

general_criteria: The app/action must be fully functional and publicly accessible
Must have a clear README with setup instructions, usage examples, and configuration
Must include documentation for all features, inputs, outputs, and permissions
Must provide support channels (e.g., GitHub Issues, Discord, or email)
Must comply with GitHub Marketplace Terms of Service
Must complete the Marketplace verification process (for paid listings)
Must not violate GitHub's Acceptable Use Policies
Must have a clear pricing model (Free, Free with paid tiers, or Paid)
security_requirements: Must use HTTPS for all communications
Must securely store any tokens, keys, or secrets
Must follow OAuth best practices if using user authentication
Must validate webhook payloads using a secret token
Must not expose sensitive data in logs or error messages

steps.action: Create a GitHub App or GitHub Action in a public repository
steps.details: Go to GitHub Developer Settings > GitHub Apps > New GitHub App. Configure name, homepage URL, callback URL, webhook URL, permissions, and events.
Ensure the app/action works correctly with GitHub APIs. Use a staging environment or test repository.
Write a comprehensive README with setup instructions, configuration options, examples, and troubleshooting.

For GitHub Apps: Go to your app settings and click 'Publish to Marketplace'. Fill in listing details (description, screenshots, pricing). For Actions: Go to the repository's Actions tab and click 'Publish to Marketplace'.
For paid listings, GitHub reviews the app for quality, security, and documentation standards. This may take several business days.
Regularly update the app/action, respond to issues, and keep documentation current.

ai_agent_specific_considerations: AI agents should clearly disclose their AI-powered nature in the listing description
Must document what data the AI agent collects, processes, and stores
Should provide opt-in/opt-out mechanisms for AI analysis features
Must comply with data privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA, etc.)
Consider whether the AI agent runs entirely on GitHub (Actions) or requires external infrastructure (App with webhook/callback)
For Actions-based AI agents: be mindful of runtime limits (6 hours max for workflows, limited storage)
For App-based AI agents: ensure proper authentication and authorization flows
Should provide clear output/result formats that users can integrate into their workflows

pricing_options.description: Listed publicly at no cost, no payment handling required
Free tier available with optional paid upgrades for more features or usage
Requires GitHub's Marketplace verification and payment processing setup

key_takeaways: An AI agent can be listed as either a GitHub App (interactive, API-driven) or a GitHub Action (automated, CI/CD-integrated)
Both require a public repository, clear documentation, and adherence to GitHub's security standards
Paid listings require a formal verification process by GitHub
AI agents must be transparent about AI usage, data handling, and privacy
The GitHub Marketplace provides a powerful distribution channel for reaching developers directly in their workflow
